articles.title: How federal aid is recalculated when you drop below full-time status
articles.content: Dropping below full-time enrollment (typically 12 credits for undergraduates) can change your federal aid eligibility. The Pell Grant is prorated by enrollment intensity, and most federal loans require at least half-time (6 credits) status to disburse. Before you drop a class, contact the Financial Aid Office so we can model the impact on your package and protect any need-based or institutional awards. We can often suggest alternatives — an incomplete, a tutoring referral, or a special-circumstances review — that keep your aid intact.
articles.snippet: Dropping below full-time enrollment (typically 12 credits for undergraduates) can change your federal aid eligibility

articles.title: Work-study vs. on-campus employment: what counts toward your aid package
articles.content: Federal Work-Study (FWS) is need-based aid you earn through an approved part-time job; your earnings do not count against next year's FAFSA in the same way regular wages do. Standard on-campus employment is open to any student but is treated as ordinary income. If work-study appears on your award letter, you must be hired into an FWS-eligible position to use it. Talk to us before swapping jobs so we can preserve the work-study portion of your package.

articles.title: Appealing your financial aid award: the special-circumstances form
articles.content: If your family has experienced a significant change — job loss, reduced income, large medical bills, divorce, or the death of a parent — you may request a professional-judgment review using the Special Circumstances Appeal form. Submit documentation of the change and a short narrative describing your situation. Appeals are reviewed individually, and we can often increase need-based aid. Hardship appeals are handled with priority; reach out as soon as the change occurs rather than waiting for the next aid year.

articles.title: Tuition payment plans and deadlines
articles.content: The Bursar offers interest-free monthly payment plans that split each semester's balance into installments. Enrollment opens before the term begins and closes after the add/drop period. Plans require a small enrollment fee and automatic payments. Missing the payment-plan deadline may result in a registration hold, so enroll early. If you cannot meet an installment, contact the Bursar before the due date to avoid late fees and holds.
articles.snippet: The Bursar offers interest-free monthly payment plans that split each semester's balance into installments

articles.title: Loyola scholarships, Magis grants, and need-based aid stacking rules
articles.content: Institutional awards — merit scholarships, Magis grants, and need-based grants — can often be combined, but total gift aid cannot exceed your cost of attendance. When outside scholarships arrive, we may adjust loans or work-study first to keep your grants whole. Renewal usually requires full-time enrollment and a minimum GPA. Review your award letter's stacking notes and contact us before declining any award so we can show you the net effect on your package.
articles.snippet: Institutional awards — merit scholarships, Magis grants, and need-based grants — can often be combined, but total gift aid cannot exceed your cost of attendance
